title: ammannia senegalensis var. auriculata
intro: Ammannia senegalensis var. auriculata, also known as African bushwillow or bushwillow, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Lythraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ammannia senegalensis var. auriculata include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusion, decoction, and tincture, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
